suggestion:
You ran {count} grep/rg searches this session. For faster semantic code exploration:
  `mnemex --agent map "your concept"` -- understands intent, not just text
  `mnemex --agent symbol "SymbolName"` -- direct AST symbol lookup
  Skill: use the Skill tool with `code-analysis:mnemex-search`

Session files detected in /tmp/ -- these are cleared on reboot. Use persistent paths:
  `ai-docs/sessions/{task-slug}-{timestamp}-{random}/` for research artifacts
  `.claude/.coaching/` for plugin state
  See: CLAUDE.md Session Directories section

'{subagent_type}' is a Skill, not an agent -- using Task causes a triple failure cascade.
  Use the Skill tool instead: Skill({ "skill": "{subagent_type}" })
  See: CLAUDE.md Skill Routing table

You read {count} files before delegating to an agent (pre-digestion anti-pattern).
  For investigation tasks, give agents the raw problem -- they investigate independently.
  Pre-digested context reduces multi-model diversity. See: MEMORY.md 'Raw Task vs Pre-Digested Context'

Task prompt matched '{pattern}' pattern but used '{actual}' agent.
  Consider '{recommended}' -- it is optimized for this task type.
  See: CLAUDE.md Task Routing table

Code review performed by a single model. Consider multi-model consensus:
  `/team` command -- gets independent perspectives from 3+ models
  Multi-model review has documented 3-5x better issue detection vs single-model

bash_patterns.command: gh pr list, gh issue create, gh repo view

suggestion:
Detected manual {plugin} operations. The {plugin} could automate this:
  `{command}`

Feature development session detected (dev:architect + dev:developer) but multi-model review was skipped.
  Phase 3 and Phase 5 of /dev:feature require model selection via AskUserQuestion and external reviews via claudish.
  Next time, ensure model selection is not skipped -- multi-model review catches 3-5x more issues than single-model.

Detected {count} sequential Task calls that may be parallelizable.
  If tasks are independent, add `run_in_background: true` to all but the last:
  Task({ subagent_type: "...", run_in_background: true, ... })
  Parallel execution can reduce total time significantly.

You ran {count} test/dev-server commands via Bash. These work better with the terminal plugin:
  `/terminal:run npm test` -- real TTY, proper rendering, captured output
  `/terminal:watch npm run dev` -- long-running with 'ready' detection
  `/terminal:repl psql` -- interactive database shells
  Terminal plugin provides screen-aware output, keystroke injection, and split-pane TDD loops.

You called WebFetch {count} times this session. For JavaScript-heavy SPAs, form interactions, or structured scraping, the browser-use plugin gives full browser control:
  `mcp__browser-use__browser_navigate` -- navigate with full JS rendering
  `mcp__browser-use__browser_extract_content` -- structured extraction
  `mcp__browser-use__browser_screenshot` -- capture live screenshots
  Skills: `browser-use:web-scraping`, `browser-use:navigation-patterns`

claudish was run directly in a Bash call. This pollutes the context window with 10K+ tokens.
  Always run claudish inside a sub-agent via the Task tool with run_in_background: true.
  Read the skill first: Skill({ "skill": "multimodel:claudish-usage" })
  Pattern: Task({ subagent_type: "dev:developer", run_in_background: true, prompt: "run: claudish ..." })

You edited .claude/settings.json directly. claudeup provides an interactive TUI for this:
  `npx claudeup` -- add marketplaces, install plugins, configure MCP servers, set up statusline
  No install needed -- just run `pnpx claudeup` or `npm install -g claudeup`
  Much safer than manual JSON editing (validates structure, avoids syntax errors).

Detected raw {plugin} CLI usage. The {plugin} provides structured workflows:
  `{command}` -- orchestrated with multi-agent coordination, error handling, and format management
  Skills: `video-editing:ffmpeg-core`, `video-editing:transcription`, `video-editing:final-cut-pro`

You ran {count} Grep searches with no mnemex calls. For codebase investigation, mnemex provides semantic understanding:
  Skill({ "skill": "code-analysis:mnemex-search" }) -- before any mnemex usage
  `/code-analysis:setup` -- index your codebase for AST-aware search
  `mnemex callers <function>` -- find all callers without grep
  `mnemex map <concept>` -- semantic concept search across the codebase

You read {count} files sequentially -- a common sign of manual codebase tracing. mnemex can map this automatically:
  `mnemex dependency-graph <module>` -- visualize module dependencies
  `mnemex callers <function>` -- all callers in one call
  `mnemex callees <function>` -- all callees in one call
  Skill({ "skill": "code-analysis:investigate" }) -- routes to the right investigation mode

You used /plugin commands to install plugins. claudeup provides a full interactive TUI for this:
  `npx claudeup` -- browse and install plugins, configure MCP servers, set up statusline
  Features: marketplace browsing, MCP server registry, statusline presets
  No installation needed: `pnpx claudeup` works immediately.

External model names detected in task prompts but claudish was not used. claudish lets Claude Code run any model via OpenRouter:
  Install: `npm install -g claudish`
  Set: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your-key`
  Use `/team` command for parallel multi-model reviews
  See: `multimodel:claudish-usage` skill for invocation patterns

UI implementation detected (dev:frontend) but no design review was performed.
  `/designer:review` -- pixel-diff comparison against Figma or reference screenshots
  `/designer:ui` -- Gemini multimodal accessibility and usability review
  Catching design drift early prevents rework. designer:compare shows pixel-level differences.

Agent or command file written directly without agentdev workflow. This skips design validation and quality review:
  `/agentdev:develop` -- full cycle: architect -> plan -> implement -> review
  Without this, agents often have schema violations, missing XML standards, or poor skill boundaries.
  Use `agentdev:reviewer` skill or agentdev:reviewer agent to validate existing files.

Complex feature session detected (5+ developer tasks) without Conductor tracking.
  `/conductor:new-track` -- create a hierarchical plan with spec, phases, and TDD commits
  `/conductor:status` -- see progress across all tracks
  `/conductor:revert` -- logical undo at track/phase/task level (not just git)
  Conductor maintains context across sessions so you never lose 'where we left off'.

External image generation tool mentioned ({plugin} reference). The nanobanana plugin provides image generation directly in Claude Code:
  `/nanobanana:generate "description"` -- text-to-image via Gemini 3 Pro Image
  `/nanobanana:style` -- create reusable style templates for consistent outputs
  `/nanobanana:edit` -- modify existing images with natural language
  Requires: `GEMINI_API_KEY` environment variable.

SEO tool references detected. The seo plugin provides a full autonomous SEO toolkit:
  `/seo:start` -- interactive workflow router for any SEO goal
  `/seo:research` -- keyword research with multi-agent orchestration
  `/seo:optimize` -- content optimization with E-E-A-T validation
  `/seo:audit` -- technical SEO: crawlability, Core Web Vitals, schema markup
  `~80% autonomy` with AUTO GATEs for self-correction.

Browser automation framework detected ({plugin} reference). The browser-use plugin provides 18 MCP tools directly in Claude Code:
  `mcp__browser-use__browser_navigate` -- navigate URLs
  `mcp__browser-use__browser_click` -- click elements
  `mcp__browser-use__browser_extract_content` -- extract structured content
  `mcp__browser-use__browser_screenshot` -- capture screenshots
  Skills: `browser-use:web-scraping`, `browser-use:debug-ui`

CLAUDE.md was written manually. The dev plugin can scaffold a complete project context:
  `/dev:setup` -- interactive setup: task routing table, agent delegation rules, stack detection
  `/dev:help` -- shows detected stack and all available commands
  A proper CLAUDE.md with routing table significantly improves agent delegation accuracy.

TUI application or database shell invoked via Bash -- these require a real TTY to render correctly.
  `/terminal:tui {app}` -- full-screen TUI navigation with keystroke injection
  `/terminal:repl {shell}` -- database REPLs with proper prompt detection and clean exit
  The terminal plugin uses ht-mcp for isolated PTY sessions and tmux-mcp for workspace layouts.
